description: A simple blog application with user registration, login, and authenticated posting capabilities. All data stored in-memory (no database).

tasks.task: Create the authentication module with in-memory user storage. Create src/auth/ folder with: (1) auth.module.ts - NestJS module for auth, (2) auth.controller.ts with POST /auth/register (accepts email, password, name) and POST /auth/login (accepts email, password, returns token), (3) auth.service.ts with in-memory user Map storage, password hashing using crypto, and simple JWT-like token generation (base64 encoded JSON with expiry), (4) auth.guard.ts - a CanActivate guard that validates the Authorization Bearer token, (5) user.interface.ts with User type (id, email, name, passwordHash, createdAt). Register the AuthModule in app.module.ts. Enable CORS in main.ts for frontend access.

Create the posts module with in-memory storage. Create src/posts/ folder with: (1) posts.module.ts - NestJS module importing AuthModule for guards, (2) posts.controller.ts with GET /posts (public, returns all posts with author info), POST /posts (protected, creates post for authenticated user), DELETE /posts/:id (protected, only author can delete), (3) posts.service.ts with in-memory Map storage for posts, (4) post.interface.ts with Post type (id, title, content, authorId, authorName, createdAt). All protected routes should use the AuthGuard from auth module. Register PostsModule in app.module.ts.

Create the authentication context and API service. Create: (1) src/api.ts - API helper with base URL http://localhost:3000, functions for register(email, password, name), login(email, password), getPosts(), createPost(title, content, token), deletePost(id, token), (2) src/AuthContext.tsx - React context with AuthProvider component managing user state (user object with id, email, name, token) in localStorage, providing login/logout/register functions and isAuthenticated boolean, (3) src/types.ts with TypeScript interfaces for User, Post, LoginResponse, RegisterResponse.

Create the authentication UI components. Create: (1) src/components/LoginForm.tsx - form with email and password fields, login button, link to register, error display, uses AuthContext, (2) src/components/RegisterForm.tsx - form with name, email, password fields, register button, link to login, error display, uses AuthContext, (3) src/components/Header.tsx - shows app title 'Simple Blog', and either login/register buttons or user name with logout button based on auth state.

Create the blog posts UI components and update App. Create: (1) src/components/PostList.tsx - fetches and displays all posts, shows author name and date, shows delete button only for posts by current user, (2) src/components/CreatePostForm.tsx - form with title and content fields, only shown when authenticated, calls createPost API, (3) Update src/App.tsx to be the main blog app: import AuthProvider, render Header, conditionally show LoginForm or RegisterForm based on route/state, show CreatePostForm for authenticated users, always show PostList. Add simple client-side routing state (view: 'login' | 'register' | 'blog'). Update src/style.css with styles for forms, posts, header, and blog layout.

exfe: Navigate to homepage, verify blog posts list is visible (empty initially)
Click register, fill form with valid data, submit - verify successful registration and auto-login
Verify header shows logged in user name and logout button
Create a new post with title and content, verify it appears in the list with correct author
Verify delete button appears on own posts, click delete, verify post is removed
Click logout, verify header shows login/register buttons again
Login with previously registered credentials, verify successful login
Verify create post form is visible when logged in, hidden when logged out
